forbidden_credentials_or_material: unsafe primary bearer-token storage for browser writes
boundaries.label: Interactive browser human boundary
boundaries.principal_resolution: Human principal resolved server-side before route composition, stream issuance, upload-session allocation, or command acceptance.
boundaries.session_carrier: Secure browser session cookie or equivalent same-origin protected session plus anti-CSRF binding.
boundaries.step_up_support: Yes; contained escalation path with session challenge rotation.
boundaries.storage_boundary: Backend session store plus browser-safe derivable cache only.

forbidden_credentials_or_material: sensitive mutation authority before authenticated upgrade
scope widening from deep-link context alone
boundaries.label: Portal invite or deep-link pre-upgrade boundary
boundaries.principal_resolution: Context may be known, but sensitive mutation authority is not live until authenticated upgrade completes.
boundaries.session_carrier: Contextual route and return-path state only.
boundaries.step_up_support: Only after authenticated upgrade into the normal portal session.
boundaries.storage_boundary: Local route context only; no trust-sensitive mutation state.

forbidden_credentials_or_material: unrestricted embedded credential surfaces for primary sign-in or step-up
boundaries.label: Interactive native human boundary
boundaries.principal_resolution: Human principal resolved through system-browser or platform-auth-session sign-in and server-side actor-session validation.
boundaries.session_carrier: ASWebAuthenticationSession or equivalent plus Keychain-backed product-session material.
boundaries.step_up_support: Yes; via system-browser or platform auth session with challenge rotation on completion.
boundaries.storage_boundary: Keychain and OS-protected tenant-bound cache only.

allowed_credentials_or_material: short-lived non-browser bearer or client credential
boundaries.label: Machine automation boundary
boundaries.principal_resolution: Service principal or external automation identity resolved per command; never treated as an interactive human session.
boundaries.session_carrier: Short-lived machine credential and explicit `command_id` plus `idempotency_key`.
boundaries.step_up_support: No; machine actors cannot satisfy human step-up or approval paths.
boundaries.storage_boundary: Managed runtime secret boundary or vault-backed credential retrieval.

boundaries.label: Embedded web-view boundary
boundaries.principal_resolution: Not trusted as the primary sign-in, step-up, or authority handoff authority.
boundaries.session_carrier: Low-risk documentation or help content only.
boundaries.storage_boundary: No sensitive session authority should be anchored here.

allowed_credentials_or_material: vault-held IdP client secrets or private keys
forbidden_credentials_or_material: raw authority access or refresh tokens in browser, queue, read model, or device cache
mixed storage of IdP tenant-admin material with general application state
boundaries.label: Authority-token and IdP secret boundary
boundaries.principal_resolution: Interactive and machine identities may trigger authority work, but raw token and client-secret material stays in governed secret boundaries.
boundaries.session_carrier: Vault-held authority tokens, IdP admin material, and client-secret records referenced indirectly from application flows.
boundaries.step_up_support: Not applicable directly; this boundary exists to keep downstream token handling governed.
